mnemonics: C.L.O.U.D.S. (Consolidated, Lower-cost, Optimized, Ubiquitous, Dynamic, Sustainable)
keyPoints: Resource consolidation through virtualization reduces the total number of physical servers required.
Dynamic provisioning allows for scaling resources on-demand, avoiding over-provisioning and waste.
High utilization rates and energy-efficient data centers significantly lower the carbon footprint per computation.
commonMistakes: Assuming cloud computing is inherently 'green' without considering the energy source of the provider's data centers.
Overlooking the carbon cost of data transmission and network infrastructure.
Believing that migrating to the cloud automatically reduces an organization's IT energy consumption, without proper architecture and resource management.
